sources.title: Prevention, Screening, Diagnosis, and Treatment of Iron Deficiency and Iron Deficiency Anemia in Infants, Children, and Adolescents: Clinical Report
supports: CBC and ferritin as core diagnostic testing for iron deficiency anemia
Age- and sex-specific fallback hemoglobin, MCV, and RDW intervals for 6 months to <18 years
Ferritin thresholds of ≤20 ng/mL in young/school-aged children and ≤30 ng/mL in adolescents and all menstruating patients
Ferritin interpretation with inflammation and CRP
sTfR/log10(ferritin) index interpretation
Microcytic differentiation among iron deficiency, thalassemia, and anemia of inflammation
Severe iron deficiency anemia category at hemoglobin <7 g/dL (not a universal transfusion threshold)

sourceLocator.raw: Screening > Laboratory Screening (paragraph beginning 'Screening to identify children and adolescents with ID and IDA includes')

passages.exactPassage: AAP names the CBC (Hb, MCV, RDW) plus serum ferritin as the two core laboratory tests for identifying iron deficiency and IDA, with both performed together.

applicability.age: Infants, children, and adolescents (birth through adolescence) being screened for ID/IDA; general primary-care and subspecialty context
applicability.assay: CBC on an automated cell counter; serum ferritin immunoassay. Not a single numeric threshold — test-selection statement

passages.supersedes: 2010 AAP clinical report on screening/prevention of ID and IDA
passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Table 1 — 'Normal Hematologic Parameters in Infants, Children, and Adolescents by Age and Sex, From Age 9 Months to 18 Years' (ref 42), Hemoglobin / MCV / RDW rows; columns 6–24 mo, 2–6 y, 6–12 y, 12–<18 y (Females, Males)

passages.exactPassage: Table 1 provides age- and sex-specific normal Hb (g/dL), MCV (fL), and RDW (%) intervals spanning 6–24 months through 12–<18 years, used as the KB fallback CBC reference intervals.

applicability.age: Females and males, 6–24 mo / 2–6 y / 6–12 y / 12–<18 y (table title states 'From Age 9 Months to 18 Years'); age- and sex-stratified
applicability.assay: Automated CBC analyzer; intervals derived from reference source cited as ref 42. Local laboratory intervals should be used where available

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (final sentence of paragraph reviewing SF thresholds, following WHO and ASH threshold discussion)

passages.exactPassage: AAP sets serum ferritin ≤20 ng/mL as indicating ID for young and school-aged children and ≤30 ng/mL for adolescents and all menstruating individuals.

applicability.age: Young and school-aged children (≤20 ng/mL) vs adolescents and all menstruating individuals (≤30 ng/mL); age- and menstruating-status stratified
applicability.assay: Serum ferritin (SF) immunoassay; interpretation confounded by inflammation (see ev_004). AAP threshold differs from WHO <12/<15 ng/mL and aligns with ASH ≤20/≤30 ng/mL

passages.supersedes: Longstanding SF thresholds of 10–12 ng/mL; WHO 2020 <12/<15 ng/mL cited as alternative provenance
passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph beginning 'In most otherwise healthy children without evidence of acute infection or chronic disease')

passages.exactPassage: Ferritin is an acute-phase reactant; an elevated CRP in an ill or inflamed child means SF cannot be treated as a reliable ID marker, while CRP is unnecessary in a clinically well child.

applicability.age: Children/adolescents with acute or chronic inflammation, tissue injury, or acute illness; contrasted with clinically well children
applicability.assay: Serum ferritin interpreted alongside C-reactive protein (CRP); qualitative interpretive rule, no numeric CRP cutoff stated

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Diagnosis > Iron Studies (paragraph on soluble transferrin receptor); corroborated by Table 4 (sTfR1) row

passages.exactPassage: The sTfR-ferritin index (sTfR/log10 ferritin) is elevated (>2) in iron deficiency and <1 in anemia of chronic inflammation without concomitant ID, helping distinguish the two.

applicability.age: Children with chronic inflammatory conditions and risk factors for ID; used to separate ID from anemia of chronic inflammation
applicability.assay: Soluble transferrin receptor (sTfR/sTfR1) assay combined with serum ferritin; index = sTfR/log10(ferritin). Less affected by inflammation than SF; limited availability and lack of published pediatric norms

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Laboratory Diagnosis of IDA > Complete Blood Cell Count (paragraph beginning 'The most valuable laboratory test'); Table 3 'Differential Diagnosis of Children With Microcytic Anemia'

passages.exactPassage: Microcytic anemia with reduced MCV and elevated RDW is most consistent with IDA, but thalassemia trait and chronic inflammation can also cause microcytic anemia and must be differentiated (Table 3).

applicability.age: Children/adolescents with microcytic anemia; differential across IDA, alpha/beta thalassemia trait, and anemia of inflammation/chronic disease
applicability.assay: CBC (MCV, RDW) plus SF/iron panel; beta thalassemia trait via elevated Hb A2 on Hb analysis after 6 months of age (ID falsely normalizes Hb A2); qualitative pattern, not a single cutoff

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Treatment of Severe or Refractory IDA > Severe IDA (opening sentences)

passages.exactPassage: AAP defines severe IDA as confirmed IDA with Hb <7 g/dL, and notes some such children are minimally symptomatic — transfusion is reserved for clinical instability, not triggered by the Hb value alone.

applicability.age: Young children and adolescents with confirmed IDA; category applies within an established IDA diagnosis, not to anemia of any cause
applicability.assay: Hemoglobin from CBC; category is diagnostic-severity classification, not an executable transfusion rule (transfusion tied to hemodynamic instability / blood loss, not the number alone)

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

passages.surveillanceQuery: AAP iron deficiency and iron deficiency anemia clinical report update after 2026

sources.title: Guideline on haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations
supports: Use of current age/population-appropriate hemoglobin cutoffs
Recognition that physiologic and contextual factors affect hemoglobin interpretation
Need for transparent cutoff provenance rather than a single universal pediatric threshold

sourceLocator.raw: Executive summary, p.xi, Table 2 (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define anaemia in individuals and populations)

passages.exactPassage: WHO 2024 defines anaemia with age/population-specific haemoglobin cutoffs, including four distinct pediatric bands from 6 months to 14 years.

applicability.age: Children 6 months-14 years, both sexes; age-banded (6-23 mo, 24-59 mo, 5-11 y, 12-14 y girls/boys); apparently-healthy reference basis; applies at individual and population level
applicability.assay: Cutoffs defined on haemoglobin concentration via a 5th-percentile statistical derivation; stated as not tied to a specific analyzer but assumes standardized haemoglobin measurement

passages.supersedes: WHO 2011 'Haemoglobin concentrations for the diagnosis of anaemia and assessment of severity' and prior WHO documents (1968-2005)
passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Executive summary, p.xi, Normative statement 1.a (Haemoglobin cutoffs to define any anaemia)

passages.exactPassage: The 2024 revision modified the haemoglobin cutoff for exactly one pediatric group -- children 6-23 months -- rather than applying a single universal pediatric threshold.

applicability.age: Children 6-23 months, both sexes -- the only pediatric group whose defining cutoff was changed in the 2024 revision
applicability.assay: 5th-percentile statistical cutoff derivation; stated as not method-dependent

passages.supersedes: Prior WHO 6-23 mo cutoff (explicit prior numeric value not stated verbatim in executive summary -- see Limitations)
passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Section 1 (What should be the haemoglobin cutoffs...), scope/definitions narrative, para 'Physiological, environmental, and genetic factors...'

passages.exactPassage: WHO states physiological, environmental, and genetic factors and lifecycle variation must be considered when defining haemoglobin cutoffs -- supporting contextual, non-universal interpretation.

applicability.age: All age and lifecycle groups including children (explicitly 'early life ... and with age'); context = defining/interpreting haemoglobin cutoffs
applicability.assay: not applicable (qualitative interpretive principle)

passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Executive summary, p.xii-xiii, Normative statement 2.a.1 (Elevation of residence) + Table 4 and equation footnote b

passages.exactPassage: WHO recommends adjusting haemoglobin to define anaemia for elevation of residence, via a stated increment table and quadratic equation -- a contextual/physiologic modifier of interpretation.

applicability.age: All population groups including children; residents/workers at elevation, esp. below 2500 m (>98% of world population)
applicability.assay: Adjustment applied to the haemoglobin concentration value (subtract from observed Hb, or add to the cutoff); not analyzer-dependent

passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Executive summary, p.xiv, Normative statement 2.b (Infection and inflammation)

passages.exactPassage: WHO explicitly recommends that haemoglobin NOT be adjusted for infection/inflammation -- a contextual caveat distinguishing haemoglobin interpretation from inflammation-adjusted iron markers.

applicability.age: All groups including children in infection/inflammation- and malaria-endemic settings
applicability.assay: Haemoglobin is NOT inflammation-adjusted (contrast: serum ferritin / transferrin receptor commonly are); not analyzer-dependent

passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Introduction / methods narrative, para 'This guideline updates and complements previous recommendations...'

passages.exactPassage: WHO documents that the 2024 cutoffs update and consolidate a traceable lineage of prior WHO guidance (1968-2005), giving transparent provenance for the thresholds.

applicability.age: not applicable (provenance / methodology statement)
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Five WHO documents 1968-2005; WHO 2011 summary publication
passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

passages.surveillanceQuery: WHO haemoglobin cutoffs anaemia guideline update after 2024

sources.title: Anemia in the pediatric patient
supports: Morphology-first and reticulocyte-response framework for pediatric anemia
Broad differential for microcytic, normocytic, and macrocytic anemia
Peripheral smear and multi-lineage cytopenia interpretation
Hemolysis, marrow production failure, nutritional, renal, endocrine, infectious, and inherited causes

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Classification of anemia' (refers to Figure 2)

passages.exactPassage: The review's core framework classifies pediatric anemia first by MCV (microcytic/normocytic/macrocytic) then refines by reticulocyte count — the morphology-first, reticulocyte-response approach the KB relies on.

applicability.age: Infants and children with anemia (individual-patient diagnostic workup); not applicable to a single numeric cutoff
applicability.assay: Automated hematology analyzer (MCV) plus reticulocyte count; classification is method-dependent on analyzer-reported indices

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Normocytic anemia' (first sentence)

passages.exactPassage: The review anchors the normocytic band at MCV 80-100 fL, the only explicit numeric MCV range stated in the source.

applicability.age: Pediatric patients (age band not sub-specified in this sentence); note the review's own Figure 1/Table 1 show MCV reference limits are age-dependent in children, so this 80-100 fL band is a general anchor, not an age-specific cutoff
applicability.assay: Automated hematology analyzer-reported MCV; method-dependent

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Laboratory investigation'

passages.exactPassage: Reticulocyte count direction (elevated vs low relative to anemia severity) distinguishes an adequate erythropoietic response to blood loss/hemolysis from inadequate marrow production.

applicability.age: Infants and children with anemia; interpretive rule, not applicable to a single sex/age band
applicability.assay: Reticulocyte count (automated or manual); interpretation is relative to degree of anemia, no fixed numeric cutoff given

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Laboratory investigation' (peripheral blood smear)

passages.exactPassage: Peripheral smear morphology (spherocytes, elliptocytes, schistocytes/helmet cells) points to specific hemolytic mechanisms — supporting the KB's smear-interpretation use of this source.

applicability.age: Infants and children with anemia; qualitative smear findings, not applicable to a numeric cutoff
applicability.assay: Peripheral blood smear microscopy (morphology); not a numeric threshold

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'BM replacement syndromes'

passages.exactPassage: Multilineage cytopenia — pancytopenia with relative reticulocytopenia, teardrop RBCs, giant platelets, and immature leukocytes — signals marrow replacement, backing the KB's multi-lineage-cytopenia escalation.

applicability.age: Infants and children with anemia plus another cytopenia; qualitative pattern, not applicable to a numeric cutoff
applicability.assay: CBC differential plus peripheral blood smear; not a numeric threshold

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

passages.surveillanceQuery: Anemia in the pediatric patient (Blood 2022) update or successor review after 2022

sources.title: Recommended Actions Based on Blood Lead Level
sources.organization: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
supports: CDC blood lead reference value of 3.5 µg/dL
Venous confirmation after elevated capillary screening
Urgency tiers and confirmation timelines for higher blood lead levels
Assessment and treatment of coexisting iron deficiency

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level' / heading 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 3.5 micrograms per deciliter'

passages.exactPassage: CDC's Blood Lead Reference Value (BLRV) is 3.5 µg/dL; a BLL at or above this value triggers the recommended-action pathway.

applicability.age: Children (childhood lead poisoning prevention); both sexes; CDC screening context cites Medicaid-enrolled testing at ages 12 and 24 months, or 24-72 months if never screened
applicability.assay: Blood lead level measured in µg/dL; capillary (finger-prick/heel-prick) or venous sample for initial screening; capillary result at/above BLRV requires a venous sample

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'Initial screening blood lead level', first paragraph

passages.exactPassage: A capillary screening result at or above the BLRV must be confirmed with a venous sample before further action.

applicability.age: Children undergoing initial blood lead screening; both sexes
applicability.assay: Method-dependent: capillary (finger/heel-prick) initial screen vs venous confirmatory draw; venous draw recommended for confirmatory BLL screening

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sourceLocator.raw: Table 1 — 'Recommended schedule for obtaining a confirmatory venous sample'

passages.exactPassage: CDC Table 1 sets confirmatory-venous-sample timelines that shorten as the initial capillary BLL rises: ≥3.5-9 within 3 months, 10-19 within 1 month, 20-44 within 2 weeks, ≥45 within 48 hours.

applicability.age: Children with an elevated initial screening capillary BLL; both sexes
applicability.assay: Timelines keyed to initial screening capillary BLL; confirmation is by venous sample

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sourceLocator.raw: Section 'If the patient's BLL is 3.5-19 micrograms per deciliter', iron-deficiency bullet

passages.exactPassage: For a confirmed BLL of 3.5-19 µg/dL, CDC directs providers to test for and treat iron deficiency per AAP guidelines.

applicability.age: Children with confirmed BLL 3.5-19 µg/dL; both sexes
applicability.assay: Iron-deficiency testing/treatment thresholds deferred to AAP guidelines; not specified numerically in this CDC document

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sourceLocator.raw: Headings 'If the patient's BLL is 20-44 micrograms per deciliter' and 'If the patient's BLL is ≥ 45 micrograms per deciliter'

passages.exactPassage: CDC defines escalating action tiers at 20-44 µg/dL (history/physical, environmental investigation, PEHSU/Poison Control contact) and ≥45 µg/dL (highest tier: detailed neuro exam, possible hospital admission, toxicologist consult for chelation).

applicability.age: Children with confirmed BLL 20-44 µg/dL or ≥45 µg/dL; both sexes
applicability.assay: Tiers keyed to confirmed venous BLL; not analyzer-specific in this document

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

passages.surveillanceQuery: CDC blood lead reference value and recommended actions update after 2025-08-21

sources.title: Clinical Decision Support Software: Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff
supports: Non-device CDS criteria under section 520(o)(1)(E) of the FD&C Act
Need for a health care professional to independently review the basis of recommendations
Regulatory importance of intended use, time criticality, and specificity of outputs

sourceLocator.raw: Section II (Background), four-criteria enumeration (pp. 5-6)

passages.exactPassage: FDA states a CDS software function is excluded from the device definition under section 520(o)(1)(E) only if it meets all four listed non-device CDS criteria.

applicability.age: not applicable
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance, September 28, 2022
passages.surveillanceQuery: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance update after 2026-01-29

sourceLocator.raw: Section II (Background), Criterion (4) verbatim (p. 6); also Section IV(4), pp. 14-15

passages.exactPassage: Criterion 4 requires the software be intended to enable the health care professional to independently review the basis for the recommendations so the HCP does not rely primarily on them.

applicability.age: not applicable
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance, September 28, 2022
passages.surveillanceQuery: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance update after 2026-01-29

sourceLocator.raw: Section IV(3) (Interpretation of Criterion 3), pp. 10-11

passages.exactPassage: A software function that provides a specific preventive, diagnostic, or treatment output or directive fails Criterion 3, making specificity of the output a decisive regulatory factor.

applicability.age: not applicable
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance, September 28, 2022
passages.surveillanceQuery: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance update after 2026-01-29

sourceLocator.raw: Section IV(4)(a) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 14

passages.exactPassage: FDA recommends the software/labeling state the intended use, and does not consider software intended for a critical, time-sensitive task or decision to meet Criterion 4 because the HCP lacks time for independent review.

applicability.age: not applicable
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance, September 28, 2022
passages.surveillanceQuery: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance update after 2026-01-29

sourceLocator.raw: Section IV(4) (Interpretation of Criterion 4), p. 15

passages.exactPassage: FDA weighs both the level of software automation and the time-critical nature of the HCP's decision making (automation bias) when deciding whether a function permits independent review.

applicability.age: not applicable
applicability.assay: not applicable

passages.supersedes: Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance, September 28, 2022
passages.surveillanceQuery: FDA Clinical Decision Support Software final guidance update after 2026-01-29

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

sources.title: Laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency: A British Society for Haematology Guideline
supports: Quantitative enzyme testing for G6PD deficiency
Risk of a falsely normal assay during or soon after acute hemolysis or transfusion
Repeat testing when clinical suspicion remains
sources.recencyNote: Retained as a foundational laboratory guideline because no newer replacement was identified in the prioritized review set.

sourceLocator.raw: Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (first two bullets)

passages.exactPassage: G6PD deficiency should be diagnosed by measuring enzyme activity directly with a quantitative spectrophotometric assay, and a quantitative assay must follow any abnormal or borderline screening test.

applicability.age: All patients suspected of G6PD deficiency (males and females; applies across ages including neonates/children); not age-band-restricted
applicability.assay: Quantitative spectrophotometric G6PD activity assay (WHO one-substrate or Glock & McLean method); screening/POC tests are insufficient to confirm or exclude, especially in females

passages.supersedes: The Assessment of Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase Deficiency, General Haematology Task Force, 1991
passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation' (opening paragraph)

passages.exactPassage: Because reticulocytes and young red cells have much higher G6PD activity than mature cells, an assay run during or soon after a hemolytic episode can give a false-normal result.

applicability.age: Any patient tested during or soon after acute hemolysis/reticulocytosis (all ages); heightened for African-type variant
applicability.assay: Quantitative G6PD activity assay; interpretation is reticulocyte-dependent — result must be read alongside the reticulocyte count on the same sample

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 6, 'Factors affecting clinical interpretation'; corroborated by Page 5, 'Recommendations: Laboratory diagnosis' (third bullet)

passages.exactPassage: When G6PD deficiency is still suspected and no other cause of hemolysis is found, the assay should be repeated at least 2-3 months after the hemolytic episode resolves so a deficiency is not missed.

applicability.age: Patient with clinically suspected G6PD deficiency and unexplained hemolysis, initial assay normal/equivocal (all ages)
applicability.assay: Repeat quantitative G6PD activity assay after reticulocytosis resolves

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 7, 'Molecular diagnosis' (opening paragraph)

passages.exactPassage: In a recently transfused patient a G6PD deficiency may be masked, so molecular analysis should be considered when a precise diagnosis is needed.

applicability.age: Recently transfused patients, heterozygous females, or cases where reticulocytosis may mask deficiency (all ages)
applicability.assay: Enzyme activity assay may be unreliable post-transfusion (donor cells) or with reticulocytosis; molecular/DNA analysis is the confirmatory alternative

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 3, Table I 'Classification of G6PD variants' (Class / Residual G6PD activity (% of normal))

passages.exactPassage: The guideline classifies G6PD variants by residual enzyme activity as a percentage of normal — Class I and II below 10%, Class III 10-60%, Class IV 100%, Class V above 100%.

applicability.age: Hemizygous males (footnote: '*Levels of residual G6PD activity in hemizygous males'); classification framework applies across ages
applicability.assay: Residual activity as % of the laboratory's normal (quantitative assay); Class I defined clinically (CNSHA), not purely biochemically

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 6, 'Normal values' (adult reference values paragraph)

passages.exactPassage: The guideline gives adult normal-range G6PD activity reference values in international units per gram of haemoglobin at 30 C and 37 C.

applicability.age: Adults (explicitly 'For adults'); NOT applicable to neonates/infants, whose values deviate (see ev_007). Each lab should establish its own reference range (ICSH or commercial kit).
applicability.assay: Quantitative spectrophotometric assay; values are temperature-dependent (30 C vs 37 C) and 6-PGD-correction-dependent; expressed per gram haemoglobin (iu = umol substrate converted per minute)

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Page 6, 'Normal values' (newborn/premature paragraph)

passages.exactPassage: Newborns and infants have G6PD activities that deviate appreciably from the adult value (one study: newborn mean ~150% of adult mean), and activity is higher in premature infants born at 29-32 weeks gestation than in term neonates.

applicability.age: Newborns and infants; premature infants born between 29 and 32 weeks gestation vs term neonates
applicability.assay: Quantitative G6PD activity assay; adult reference ranges do not apply to neonates/infants (age- and gestational-age-dependent)

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020

sourceLocator.raw: Implementation-proposal sentinel: no located passage. This record exists so that a rule whose threshold is not mechanically traceable to a located passage in this source still resolves to something explicit rather than to nothing.

passages.surveillanceQuery: BSH laboratory diagnosis of G6PD deficiency guideline update after 2020
